{{Short description|none}} <!-- This short description is INTENTIONALLY "none" - please see WP:SDNONE before you consider changing it! -->
{{About|the men's team|the women's team|Cook Islands women's national football team}}
{{Use dmy dates|date=May 2018}}
{{Infobox National football team
| Name           = Cook Islands
| Badge          = Cook Islands FA.svg
| Badge_size     = 200px
| Nickname       = The Men in Green
| Association    = [[Cook Islands Football Association]] (CIFA)
| Confederation  = [[Oceania Football Confederation|OFC]] (Oceania)
| Captain        = [[Thane Beal]]
| Coach          = Vacant
| Most caps      = [[Tony Jamieson]] (22)
| Top scorer     = [[Taylor Saghabi]] (7)
| Home Stadium   = [[Avarua Tereora Stadium]]
| FIFA Trigramme = COK
| FIFA Rank      = {{FIFA World Rankings|COK}}
| FIFA max       = 166
| FIFA max date  = October 2015
| FIFA min       = 207
| FIFA min date  = April–July 2015
| Elo Rank       = {{World Football Elo Ratings|Cook Islands}}
| Elo max       = 170
| Elo max date  = 1971
| Elo min       = 224
| Elo min date  = 2015

| pattern_la1  = _COK2022H
| pattern_b1   = _COK2022H
| pattern_ra1  = _COK2022H
| pattern_sh1  = 
| pattern_so1  = 
| leftarm1     = 
| body1        = 
| rightarm1    = 
| shorts1      = 007C48
| socks1       = FFFFFF

| pattern_la2  = _COK2022H
| pattern_b2   = _COK2022A
| pattern_ra2  = _COK2022H
| pattern_sh2  = _whitebottom
| pattern_so2  = 
| leftarm2     = 
| body2        = 
| rightarm2    = 
| shorts2      = 007C48
| socks2       = FFFFFF

| First game         = {{nowrap|{{fb|PNG|1970}} 16–1 {{fb-rt|COK}}}}<br />([[Papeete]], [[Tahiti]]; 11 September 1971)
| Largest win        = {{fb|COK}} 3–0 {{Fb-rt|ASA}}<br />(Papeete, Tahiti; 12 June 2000) <br />
{{fb|COK}} 4–1 {{fb-rt|TUV}}<br />([[Apia]], [[Samoa]]; 1 September 2007)<br />{{fb|COK}} 3–0 {{fb-rt|KIR}}<br />([[Boulari Bay|Boulari]], [[New Caledonia]]; 1 September 2011)<br />{{fb|TON}} 0–3 {{fb-rt|COK}}<br />([[‘Atele]], [[Tonga]]; 31 August 2015) 
| Largest loss       = {{fb|TAH|1946}} 30–0 {{fb-rt|COK}}<br />(Papeete, Tahiti; 13 September 1971)
| Regional name      = [[OFC Nations Cup]]
| Regional cup apps  = 2
| Regional cup first = [[1998 OFC Nations Cup|1998]]
| Regional cup best  = Group stage ([[1998 OFC Nations Cup|1998]], [[2000 OFC Nations Cup|2000]])
|2ndRegional cup apps=5|2ndRegional name=[[Football at the Pacific Games|Pacific Games]]|2ndRegional cup first=[[Football at the 1971 South Pacific Games|1971]]|2ndRegional cup best=Sixth place ([[Football at the 1971 South Pacific Games|1971]])
|3rdRegional cup apps=2|3rdRegional name=[[Polynesia Cup]]|3rdRegional cup first=[[1998 Polynesia Cup|1998]]|3rdRegional cup best=Runners-up ([[1998 Polynesia Cup|1998]], [[2000 Polynesia Cup|2000]])}}

The '''Cook Islands men's national football team''' is the men's [[association football|football]] team that represents the [[Cook Islands]] in international competition since 1971. It is governed by the [[Cook Islands Football Association]] which is part of the [[Oceania Football Confederation]] (OFC) and [[FIFA]].

The nation has participated in seven [[FIFA World Cup]] qualification attempts since their first attempt back in 1998. They have qualified for the [[OFC Nations Cup]] twice in [[1998 OFC Nations Cup|1998]] and [[2000 OFC Nations Cup]] with both appearances seeing no wins from their four games that they have played.

==History==
===Beginnings (1971–1995)===
Despite being absent from the first three editions of the South Pacific Games, the geographical proximity of the Cook Islands with [[French Polynesia]], host of the [[Football at the 1971 South Pacific Games|1971 South Pacific Games]], allowed the Cookian team to take part in the tournament. In the first round they were defeated 16–1 by [[Papua New Guinea national football team|Papua New Guinea]] and 30–0 by [[Tahiti national football team|Tahiti]], while in the match for fifth place, they fell to [[Fiji national football team|Fiji]] 15–1.

They next played a match in 1995 when proximity allowed them to participate again in the [[Football at the 1995 South Pacific Games|1995 South Pacific Games]]. During the tournament they earned their first win, a 2–1 win over [[Wallis and Futuna national football team|Wallis and Futuna]], but lost their other four clashes resoundingly.

===The two OFC Nations Cup (1996–2001)===
The Cook Islands qualified for their first [[OFC Nations Cup]] after finishing second in the [[Polynesia Cup 1998|1998 Polynesia Cup]], which they hosted. They faced [[Australia men's national soccer team|Australia]] and [[Fiji national football team|Fiji]] in Group B, in what proved to be a tough draw. Australia crushed the Cook Islands 16–0 in their first match, scoring eight goals either side of the interval. In their next match against Fiji, however, the Cook Islands acquitted themselves much better, losing only 3–0, but with two defeats from two matches they failed to make the semi-finals.

In finishing 2nd at the [[2000 Polynesia Cup]], the Cook Islands also qualified for the [[2000 OFC Nations Cup|OFC Nations Cup]]. They were drawn in Group A along with the [[Solomon Islands national football team|Solomon Islands]], and again, [[Australia men's national soccer team|Australia]]. After their heavy defeat at the hands of Australia in the previous tournament, the Cook Islands hoped to avoid a repeat performance. However, they were powerless to prevent their opponents from running rampant, this time to the tune of 17–0. The Solomon Islands were up next, and in this game the Cook Islands had the consolation of scoring their first goal at the OFC Nations Cup – the only bright spot in an otherwise bleak 5–1 defeat. After two defeats, then, the Cooks Islands again went out at the group stage.

In 2001 during [[2002 FIFA World Cup qualification (OFC)|2002 FIFA World Cup qualification]], the Cook Islands faced [[New Zealand]], the first fixture between the two. The Cook Islands held strong, keeping the opposition level at 0-0 until half time, until substitute [[Noah Hickey]] scored a brace to put the efforts of the Cook Islands defence in vein. Goalkeeper [[Tony Jamieson]] won player of the match, making at minimum 7 and maximum of 26 saves during the match.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Soccer: All Whites turn in shocking display against lowly Cooks |url=https://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/football/soccer-all-whites-turn-in-shocking-display-against-lowly-cooks/BWGCMBG7W4XU3UJZDG7VJLU2TY/ |access-date=2026-06-27 |website=NZ Herald |language=en-NZ}}</ref>

===Decline (2002–present)===
They next entered qualification for the [[2002 OFC Nations Cup qualification|2004 tournament]] however they lost all four matches and failed to progress.

They returned to the South Pacific Games for the [[Football at the 2007 South Pacific Games|2007 competition]] where they could only beat [[Tuvalu national football team|Tuvalu]] 4–1. Four years later, at [[Football at the 2011 Pacific Games – Men's tournament|Nouméa 2011]], they defeated [[Kiribati national football team|Kiribati]] 3–0, but lost their other three meetings; while in [[2012 OFC Nations Cup qualification]] they drew with [[American Samoa national football team|American Samoa]] and fell to [[Samoa national football team|Samoa]] and [[Tonga national football team|Tonga]].

In the [[2018 FIFA World Cup qualification – OFC first round|2016 OFC Nations Cup qualifiers]], they beat [[Tonga national football team|Tonga]] 3–1 and [[Samoa national football team|Samoa]] 1–0, but fell in the last game against [[American Samoa national football team|American Samoa]] 2-0, which relegated the Cook Islands to third place, surpassed on goal difference by both Samoas.

In March 2022, the Cook Islands played their first match in seven years when they lost 2–0 to the [[Solomon Islands national football team|Solomon Islands]] in [[2022 FIFA World Cup qualification (OFC)|qualifying for the 2022 FIFA World Cup]]. However, they had to withdraw from their remaining matches due to a [[COVID-19 pandemic|COVID-19 outbreak]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Cook Islands vs Solomon Islands |url=https://elevensports.com/view/event/ckz3vfwfkupe10jfs75f85pi7 |publisher=Elevensports |date=23 March 2022 |access-date=24 March 2022}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Update on FIFA World Cup Qatar 2022 Oceania Qualifiers |url=https://www.oceaniafootball.com/update-on-fifa-world-cup-qatar-2022-oceania-qualifiers/ |publisher=Oceania Football Confederation |date=23 March 2022 |access-date=24 March 2022 |archive-date=23 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220323211813/https://www.oceaniafootball.com/update-on-fifa-world-cup-qatar-2022-oceania-qualifiers/ |url-status=live }}</ref>

On 28 August 2023, Cook Islands resumed their international matches playing against [[Tahiti national football team|Tahiti]] but suffered a 9–1 heavy defeat to Tahiti at the [[Stade Pater Te Hono Nui]] in [[Pirae]].

==Kit sponsorship==
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:left;"
!Kit supplier
!Period
|-
|align="Left"|{{flagicon|ITA}} [[Lotto Sport Italia|Lotto]] || 2001
|-
|align="Left"|{{flagicon|GER}} [[Adidas]] ||2002–2007
|-
|align="left"|{{flagicon|ITA}} [[Lotto Sport Italia|Lotto]] ||2007–2012
|-
|align="left"|{{flagicon|NZL}} Onu Football ||2016–2022
|-
|{{flagicon|AUS}} KPI Sports
|2022–2024
|-
|align="left"|{{flagicon|ITA}} [[Lotto Sport Italia]] || 2024–
|}
<small>Source:<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.oldfootballshirts.com/pt/teams/c/cook-islands/old-cook-islands-football-shirts-t2505.html |title=Antigo(a) Cook Islands camisas de futebol |access-date=4 April 2020 |archive-date=14 June 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170614132636/http://www.oldfootballshirts.com/pt/teams/c/cook-islands/old-cook-islands-football-shirts-t2505.html |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=KPI Sports|url=https://www.facebook.com/kpi.sport/posts/3268651196586503 |archive-url=https://ghostarchive.org/iarchive/facebook/614148995370083/3268651196586503 |archive-date=2022-02-26 |url-access=limited|access-date=2020-10-01|website=www.facebook.com|language=en}}{{cbignore}}</ref></small>

==Results and fixtures==
{{Main|Cook Islands national football team results}}
The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

{{legend2|#CCFFCC|Win|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Draw|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Loss|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFFF|Fixture|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

==Coaches==
{{div col}}
*{{Flagicon|COK}} Alex Napa (1996–1998)
*{{Flagicon|COK}} [[Cormac Gallen-Willcocks]] (2001–2004)
*{{Flagicon|AUS}} [[Tim Jerks]] (2004–2010)
*{{Flagicon|NZL}} [[Shane Rufer]] (2011)
*{{Flagicon|COK}} [[Paul Farrell-Turepu]] (2011–2014)
*{{Flagicon|WAL}} [[Drew Sherman]] (2015–2017)
*{{Flagicon|NZL}} [[Kevin Fallon]] (2018–2020)
*{{Flagicon|ENG}} [[Alan Taylor (footballer, born 1943)|Alan Taylor]] (2022)
*{{Flagicon|NZL}} [[Jess Ibrom]] (2023-)
{{div col end}}

==Players==

===Current squad===
The following players were called up for the [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – OFC first round|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]] match window in September 2024.<ref>{{cite web |title=Final Squad |url=https://www.facebook.com/photo/?fbid=1240747893969101&set=a.745265030184059 |website=Facebook |publisher=Cook Islands Football Association}}</ref>

''Caps and goals are correct as of 9 September 2024, after the match against [[American Samoa national football team|American Samoa]].''

{{nat fs g start}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Tahiri Elikana]]|age={{bda|1988|9|14|df=y}}|caps=13|goals=0|club=[[Papakura City FC|Papakura City]]|clubnat=NZL}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Ngereine Maro]]|age={{bda|2005|3|20|df=y}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[Auckland United FC|Auckland United]]|clubnat=NZL}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Emiel Burrow]]|age={{bda|1992|5|17|df=y}}|caps=10|goals=0|club=[[Oratia United]]|clubnat=NZL}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Thane Beal]]|age={{bda|1997|3|26|df=y}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[Westvale Olympic FC|Westvale Olympic]]|clubnat=AUS}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Orin Ruaine-Prattley]]|age={{bda|1997|11|3|df=y}}|caps=9|goals=0|club=[[Middle Park FC|Middle Park]]|clubnat=AUS}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Oscar Wichman]]|age={{bda|2005|7|1|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[Tupapa Maraerenga F.C.|Tupapa Maraerenga]]|clubnat=COK}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Tamaiva Mateariki]]|age={{bda|1999|2|5|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Nikao Sokattak F.C.|Nikao Sokattak]]|clubnat=COK}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[John Noovao]]|age={{bda|2004|9|18|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Tupapa Maraerenga F.C.|Tupapa Maraerenga]]|clubnat=COK}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Charlie Noovao-Martin]]|age={{bda|2007|7|4|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[William_Penn_University#Athletics|William Penn Statesmen Reserves]]|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Paavo Mustonen]]|age={{bda|1989|11|10|df=y}}|caps=18|goals=0|club=[[Tupapa Maraerenga F.C.|Tupapa Maraerenga]]|clubnat=COK}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Grover Harmon]]|age={{bda|1989|8|9|df=y}}|caps=18|goals=1|club=[[Tupapa Maraerenga F.C.|Tupapa Maraerenga]]|clubnat=COK}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Tupaia Ringi]]|age={{bda|2003|3|14|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Belconnen United FC|Belconnen United]]|clubnat=AUS}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Ngametua Tuakana]]|age={{bda|2005|2|28|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Tupapa Maraerenga F.C.|Tupapa Maraerenga]]|clubnat=COK}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Taci Kumsuz]]|age={{bda|1999|1|6|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=1|club=[[University of New South Wales]]|clubnat=AUS}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Taylor Saghabi]]|age={{bda|1990|12|25|df=y}}|caps=19|goals=7|club=Unattached|clubnat=AUS}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Josh Karika]]|age={{bda|1989|9|15|df=y}}|caps=7|goals=0|club=[[Oratia United]]|clubnat=NZL}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Siaosi Kaufononga]]|age={{bda|1995|2|20|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=1|club=[[Tupapa Maraerenga F.C.|Tupapa Maraerenga]]|clubnat=COK}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Tremaine Rimene-Albrett]]|age={{bda|2002|1|1|df=y}}|caps=6|goals=1|club=[[Western Suburbs FC|Western Suburbs]]|clubnat=NZL}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Dwayne Tiputoa]]|age={{bda|1997|12|8|df=y}}|caps=8|goals=1|club=[[Puaikura F.C.|Puaikura]]|clubnat=COK}}
{{National football squad end}}

===Recent call-ups===
The following players have also been called up to the Cook Islands squad within the last twelve months.

{{nat fs r start}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Sunai Joseph]]|age={{bda|1998|2|20|df=y}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[Tupapa Maraerenga F.C.|Tupapa Maraerenga]]|clubnat=COK|latest=v. {{fb|TGA}}; 26 March 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Nuku Mokoroa]]|age={{bda|2004|5|15|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Nikao Sokattak F.C.|Nikao Sokattak]]|clubnat=COK|latest=v. {{fb|TGA}}; 26 March 2024}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Lee Harmon Jr.]]|age={{bda|2001|10|23|df=y}}|caps=7|goals=0|club=[[Tupapa Maraerenga F.C.|Tupapa Maraerenga]]|clubnat=COK|latest=v. {{fb|TGA}}; 26 March 2024}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Cade Mapu]]|age={{bda|1997|12|5|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Central Coast United FC|Central Coast United]]|clubnat=AUS|latest=v. {{fb|TGA}}; 26 March 2024}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs end}}

==Player records==
{{Updated|9 September 2024}}<ref>{{cite web |title=Cook Islands |url=https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/45/Cook_Islands.html |website=National Football Teams}}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Cook Islands.''

===Most appearances===

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!width=25 |Rank
!width=150 |Name
!width=50 |Caps
!width=50 |Goals
!width=100 |Career
|-
|1
|align=left|[[Tony Jamieson]]
|22
|0
|2000–2011
|-
|2
|align=left|[[John Pareanga (footballer)|John Pareanga]]
|20
|2
|1998–2011
|-
|3
|align=left|'''[[Taylor Saghabi]]'''
|19
|7
|2011–present
|-
|rowspan=2|4
|align=left|'''[[Grover Harmon]]'''
|18
|1
|2009–present
|-
|align=left|'''[[Paavo Mustonen]]'''
|18
|0
|2007–present
|-
|6
|align=left|[[Joseph Chambers (footballer)|Joseph Chambers]]
|14
|0
|1996–2004
|-
|rowspan=3|7
|align=left|'''[[Tahiri Elikana]]'''
|13
|0
|2011–present
|-
|align=left|[[Mark Jamieson (footballer)|Mark Jamieson]]
|13
|1
|2000–2004
|-
|align=left|[[Christian Tauira]]
|13
|0
|1998–2004
|-
|rowspan=5|10
|align=left|[[Stenter Mani]]
|12
|2
|1996–2001
|-
|align=left|Joseph Miitamariki
|12
|0
|2007–2011
|-
|align=left|James Nand
|12
|0
|1996–2001
|-
|align=left|[[Daniel Shepherd]]
|12
|1
|2000–2007
|-
|align=left|[[Nikorima Te Miha]]
|12
|2
|1998–2011
|-
|}

===Top goalscorers===

{|class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!width=25 |Rank
!width=150 |Name
!width=50 |Goals
!width=50 |Caps
!width=50 |Ratio
!width=100 |Career
|-
|1
|align="left"|'''[[Taylor Saghabi]]'''
|7
|19
|{{#expr:7/19 round 2}}
|2011–present
|-
|rowspan=2|2
|align="left"|[[Joseph Ngauora]]
|3
|8
|{{#expr:3/8 round 2}}
|2009–2011
|-
|align="left"|[[Campbell Best]]
|3
|11
|{{#expr:3/11 round 2}}
|2009–2015
|-
|rowspan=4|4
|align="left"|[[Teariki Mateariki]]
|2
|6
|{{#expr:2/6 round 2}}
|2004–2007
|-
|align="left"|[[Stenter Mani]]
|2
|12
|{{#expr:2/12 round 2}}
|1996–2001
|-
|align="left"|[[Nikorima Te Miha]]
|2
|12
|{{#expr:2/12 round 2}}
|1998–2011
|-
|align="left"|[[John Pareanga (footballer)|John Pareanga]]
|2
|20
|{{#expr:2/20 round 2}}
|1998–2011
|-
|}

==Competition record==
===FIFA World Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[FIFA World Cup]]
!rowspan=39|
!colspan=7|[[FIFA World Cup qualification|Qualification]]
|-
!Year
!Host
!Round
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Games won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Games drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Games lost}}
!{{Tooltip|F|Goals scored}}
!{{Tooltip|A|Goals conceded}}
!{{Tooltip|Pos.|Position in final qualification group stage}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Games won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Games drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Games lost}}
!{{Tooltip|F|Goals scored}}
!{{Tooltip|A|Goals conceded}}
|-
|colspan=2|[[1930 FIFA World Cup|1930]] to [[1994 FIFA World Cup|1994]]
|colspan=8|''Not a FIFA member''
|colspan=7|''Not a FIFA member''
|-
|[[1998 FIFA World Cup|1998]]
|align=left|{{flag|France|1974}}
|colspan=8 rowspan=6|''Did not qualify''
|[[1998 FIFA World Cup qualification (OFC)|First round]]
|2
|0
|0
|2
|1
|4
|-
|[[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002]]
|align=left|{{flag|South Korea|1997}}<br/>{{flag|Japan}}
|[[2002 FIFA World Cup qualification (OFC)|First round]]
|4
|0
|0
|4
|2
|25
|-
|[[2006 FIFA World Cup|2006]]
|align=left|{{flag|Germany}}
|[[2006 FIFA World Cup qualification – OFC first round|First round]]
|4
|0
|0
|4
|1
|17
|-
|[[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010]]
|align=left|{{flag|South Africa}}
|[[Football at the 2007 South Pacific Games – Men's tournament|First round]]
|4
|1
|0
|3
|4
|9
|-
|[[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014]]
|align=left|{{flag|Brazil}}
|[[2014 FIFA World Cup qualification – OFC first round|First round]]
|3
|0
|1
|2
|4
|6
|-
|[[2018 FIFA World Cup|2018]]
|align=left|{{flag|Russia}}
|[[2018 FIFA World Cup qualification – OFC first round|First round]]
|3
|2
|0
|1
|4
|2
|-
|[[2022 FIFA World Cup|2022]]
|align=left|{{flag|Qatar}}
|colspan=8|''Withdrew''
|colspan=7|''Withdrew''
|-
|[[2026 FIFA World Cup|2026]]
|align=left|{{flag|Canada}}<br/>{{flag|Mexico}}<br/>{{flag|United States}}
|colspan=8 rowspan=1|''Did not qualify''
|[[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – OFC first round|First round]]
|1||0||0||1||1||3
|-
|[[2030 FIFA World Cup|2030]]
|align=left|{{flag|Morocco}}<br/>{{flag|Portugal}}<br/>{{flag|Spain}}
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|colspan=7 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|-
|[[2034 FIFA World Cup|2034]]
|align=left|{{flag|Saudi Arabia}}
|-
!colspan=2|Total
!–
!–
!–
!–
!–
!–
!–
!{{Tooltip|0/7|Number of tournaments qualified for}}
!21
!3
!1
!17
!17
!66
|}

===OFC Nations Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=10|[[OFC Men's Nations Cup|Oceania Cup / OFC Nations Cup]] record
!rowspan=39|
!colspan=6|Qualification record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!Squad
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Matches played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|New Zealand}} [[1973 OFC Nations Cup|1973]]
|colspan=9 rowspan=3|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6 rowspan=3|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|New Caledonia|french}} [[1980 OFC Nations Cup|1980]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Pacific Community}} [[1996 OFC Nations Cup|1996]]
|-
|{{flagicon|AUS}} [[1998 OFC Nations Cup|1998]]
|rowspan=2|Group stage
|6th
|2
|0
|0
|2
|0
|19
|[[1998 OFC Nations Cup squads#Cook Islands|Squad]]
|4
|2
|1
|1
|8
|11
|-
|{{flagicon|TAH}} [[2000 OFC Nations Cup|2000]]
|6th
|2
|0
|0
|2
|1
|22
|[[2000 OFC Nations Cup squads#Cook Islands|Squad]]
|4
|3
|0
|1
|8
|5
|-
|{{flagicon|NZL}} [[2002 OFC Nations Cup|2002]]
|colspan=9|''Withdrew''
|colspan=6|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|Australia}} [[2004 OFC Nations Cup|2004]]
|colspan=9 rowspan=5|''Did not qualify''
|4
|0
|0
|4
|1
|17
|-
|{{flagicon|Pacific Community}} [[2008 OFC Nations Cup|2008]]
|4
|1
|0
|3
|4
|9
|-
|{{flagicon|SOL}} [[2012 OFC Nations Cup|2012]]
|3
|0
|1
|2
|4
|6
|-
|{{flagicon|PNG}} [[2016 OFC Nations Cup|2016]]
|3
|2
|0
|1
|4
|2
|-
|{{flagicon|FIJ}} {{flagicon|VAN}} [[2024 OFC Men's Nations Cup|2024]]
|2
|1
|0
|1
|1
|1
|-
!'''Total'''
!'''Group stage'''
!'''2/11'''
!'''4'''
!'''0'''
!'''0'''
!'''4'''
!'''1'''
!'''41'''
!—
!24
!9
!2
!13
!20
!51
|}

===Pacific Games===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center"
|-
!colspan=9|[[Football at the Pacific Games|Pacific Games]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|[[Football at the 1963 South Pacific Games|1963]] to [[Football at the 1969 South Pacific Games|1969]]
|colspan=8|''did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|TAH|1946}} [[Football at the 1971 South Pacific Games|1971]]
|Group Stage
|6th
|3
|0
|0
|3
|2
|61
|-
|[[Football at the 1975 South Pacific Games|1975]] to [[Football at the 1991 South Pacific Games|1991]]
|colspan=8|''did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|TAH}} [[Football at the 1995 South Pacific Games|1995]]
|Group stage
|7th
|4
|1
|0
|3
|2
|37
|-
|{{flagicon|FIJ}} [[Men's football at the 2003 South Pacific Games|2003]]
|colspan=8|''did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|SAM}} [[Men's football at the 2007 South Pacific Games|2007]]
|rowspan=2|Group stage
|8th
|4
|1
|0
|3
|4
|9
|-
|{{flagicon|NCL|football}} [[Men's Football at the 2011 Pacific Games|2011]]
|9th
|4
|1
|0
|3
|4
|15
|-
|{{flagicon|PNG}} [[Men's Football at the 2015 Pacific Games|2015]]
|colspan=8|''N/A – tournament was U23''
|-
|{{flagicon|SAM}} [[Football at the 2019 Pacific Games – Men's tournament|2019]]
|colspan="8"|''did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|SOL}} [[Football at the 2023 Pacific Games – Men's tournament|2023]]{{refn|The Cook Islands men's national team were withdrew by the federation after the elimination from the group stage citing travelling issues for coming back from Honiara. The team didn't play the 5th place semi-final, nor the 7th place final|group=n}}
|Group stage
|8th
|2
|1
|0
|1
|2
|9
|-
!Total
!Group stage 
!5/16
!17
!4
!0
!13
!14
!131
|}

{{reflist|group=note}}

===Polynesia Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center"
|-
!colspan=9|[[Polynesia Cup]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|WSM}}[[1994 Polynesia Cup|1994]]
|colspan=8|''did not enter''
|- style="background:silver;"
|style="border: 3px solid red"|{{flagicon|COK}} [[1998 Polynesia Cup|1998]]
|Runners-up
|2nd
|4
|2
|1
|1
|8
|11
|- style="background:silver;"
|{{flagicon|TAH}} [[2000 Polynesia Cup|2000]]
|Runners-up
|2nd
|4
|3
|0
|1
|8
|5
|-
!Total
!2/3
!0 Titles
!8
!5
!1
!2
!16
!16
|}

==Head-to-head record==
{{#lst:Cook Islands national football team results|Record by opponent}}

==Historical kits==
{| class="wikitable"
|
{{Football kit 
| pattern_la  = _COK1995H
| pattern_b   = _COK1995H
| pattern_ra  = 
| pattern_sh  = 
| pattern_so  = _3_stripes_white
| leftarm     = 
| body        = 
| rightarm    = 00633A
| shorts      = 00633A
| socks       = 00633A
| title       = 1995 Home
}}
|
{{Football kit 
| pattern_la  = _COK1995A
| pattern_b   = _COK1995A
| pattern_ra  = 
| pattern_sh  = 
| pattern_so  = _3_stripes_on_white
| leftarm     = 
| body        = 
| rightarm    = FFFFFF
| shorts      = FFFFFF
| socks       = 00633A
| title       = 1995 Away
}}
|
{{Football kit 
| pattern_la  = _COK2001H
| pattern_b   = _COK2001H
| pattern_ra  = _COK2001H
| pattern_sh  = 
| pattern_so  = 
| leftarm     = 
| body        = 
| rightarm    = 
| shorts      = 00472A
| socks       = FFFFFF
| title       = 2001 Home
}}
|
{{Football kit 
| pattern_la  = _adidas_white
| pattern_b   = _Adidas_white2
| pattern_ra  = _adidas_white
| pattern_sh  = _adidaswhite
| pattern_so  = _3_stripes_white
| leftarm     = 007C48
| body        = 007C48
| rightarm    = 007C48
| shorts      = 007C48
| socks       = 007C48
| title       = 2007 Home
}}
|
{{Football kit 
| pattern_la  = _shoulder_stripes_white_shirt_alt
| pattern_b   = _3_stripes_vneck_on_white1
| pattern_ra  = _shoulder_stripes_white_shirt_alt
| pattern_sh  = _adidasshort_on_white
| pattern_so  = _3_stripes_on_white
| leftarm     = 007C48
| body        = 007C48
| rightarm    = 007C48
| shorts      = 007C48
| socks       = 007C48
| title       = 2007 Away
}}
|
{{Football kit 
| pattern_la  = _COK2011H
| pattern_b   = _COK2011H
| pattern_ra  = _COK2011H
| pattern_sh  = 
| pattern_so  = 
| leftarm     = 
| body        = 
| rightarm    = 
| shorts      = 06382B
| socks       = FFFFFF
| title       = 2011 Home
}}
|
{{Football kit 
| pattern_la  = _COK2015H
| pattern_b   = _COK2015H
| pattern_ra  = _COK2015H
| pattern_sh  = _COK2015H
| pattern_so  = 
| leftarm     = 
| body        = 
| rightarm    = 
| shorts      = 
| socks       = FFFFFF
| title       = 2015 Home
}}
|
{{Football kit 
| pattern_la  = _COK2015A
| pattern_b   = _COK2015A
| pattern_ra  = _COK2015A
| pattern_sh  = _COK2015A
| pattern_so  = 
| leftarm     = 
| body        = 
| rightarm    = 
| shorts      = 
| socks       = 007C48
| title       = 2015 Away
}}
|}
{| class="wikitable"
|
{{Football kit 
| pattern_la  = _COK2022H
| pattern_b   = _COK2022H
| pattern_ra  = _COK2022H
| pattern_sh  = 
| pattern_so  = 
| leftarm     = 
| body        = 
| rightarm    = 
| shorts      = 007C48
| socks       = FFFFFF
| title       = 2022 Home
}}
|
{{Football kit 
| pattern_la  = _COK2022H
| pattern_b   = _COK2022A
| pattern_ra  = _COK2022H
| pattern_sh  = _whitebottom
| pattern_so  = 
| leftarm     = 
| body        = 
| rightarm    = 
| shorts      = 007C48
| socks       = FFFFFF
| title       = 2022 Away
}}
|}

==Honours==
===Regional===
* '''[[Polynesia Cup]]'''
** {{Silver2}} Runners-up (2): [[1998 Polynesia Cup|1998]], [[2000 Polynesia Cup|2000]]

==See also==
* [[Cook Islands national under-20 football team]]
* [[Cook Islands national under-17 football team]]
* [[Cook Islands women's national football team]]
* [[Cook Islands women's national under-17 football team]]

== Notes ==
{{reflist|group=n}}

==References==
{{reflist}}
* {{cite web | url=https://oceaniafootballcenter.org/2022/03/19/cook-islands-and-vanuatu-out-of-world-cup-qualifiers/ | title= Cook Islands and Vanuatu out of FIFA World Cup qualifiers | date= 19 March 2022}}

==External links==
{{commonscat}}
* [https://web.archive.org/web/20150102065215/http://www.cookislandsfootball.com/ Cook Islands Football Association]
* [https://www.oceaniafootball.com/cook-islands/ Cook Islands] at [[Oceania Football Confederation|OFC]]
* [https://www.fifa.com/about-fifa/associations/COK Cook Islands] at [[FIFA]]
* [https://web.archive.org/web/20150923180225/http://www.aussiekits.com/Cook_Islands/Cook_Islands.htm Pictorial History of Cook Island Football Kits]

{{Cook Islands national football team}}
{{Cook Islands squad 1998 OFC Nations Cup}}
{{Cook Islands squad 2000 OFC Nations Cup}}
{{Football in Cook Islands}}
{{OFC teams}}
{{National sports teams of the Cook Islands}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:Cook Islands Men's National Football Team}}
[[Category:Cook Islands national football team| ]]
[[Category:Cook Islands men's national football teams| ]]
[[Category:Oceanian national association football teams]]
